title: Rune thrownaxe
wikitext:
{{External|rs=Rune throwing axe}} {{Infobox Item |name = Rune thrownaxe |image = [[File:Rune thrownaxe.png]] |release = [[29 March]] [[2004]] |update = RS2 Launched! |members = Yes |quest = No |tradeable = Yes |placeholder = Yes |equipable = Yes |stackable = Yes |noteable = No |options = Wield, Drop |examine = A finely balanced throwing axe. |value = 440 |weight = 0.000 |exchange = Yes |id = 805 }} [[File:Rune thrownaxe detail.png|left|130px]] The '''rune thrownaxe''', like all [[throwing axes]], is a [[members]]-only [[Ranged]] throwing weapon and is the second most powerful type of throwing axe, behind the [[dragon thrownaxe]]. To wield a rune throwing axe the player must have at least 40 Ranged. The [[door-support]]s that block access to lower floors of the [[Waterbirth Island Dungeon]] can be destroyed using a rune thrownaxe special attack on the middle support. As the doors usually require another player to open, players without level 85 [[Agility]]{{Boostable|yes}} for the [[Rocks_(Waterbirth_Island)|rocks shortcut]] to skip the doors often use one to reach the [[Dagannoth Kings]] conveniently. The combat achievement [[From One King to Another]] requires the player to kill [[Dagannoth Prime]] using a rune thrownaxe special attack bounced off [[Dagannoth Rex]]. ==Combat stats== {{Infobox Bonuses |astab = 0 |aslash = 0 |acrush = 0 |amagic = 0 |arange = +26 |dstab = 0 |dslash = 0 |dcrush = 0 |dmagic = 0 |drange = 0 |str = 0 |rstr = +36 |mdmg = 0 |prayer = 0 |slot = weapon |speed = 5 |attackrange = 4 |combatstyle = Thrown |image = [[File:Rune thrownaxe equipped.png|100px]] |altimage = [[File:Rune thrownaxe equipped female.png|100px]] }} {{CombatStyles|Thrown|speed=5|attackrange=4}} ==Special attack== [[File:Chainhit.gif|thumb|220px|A player performs the rune thrownaxe's special attack, ''Chainhit''.]] The rune thrownaxe has a [[special attack]], ''Chainhit'', that ricochets off the target and hits multiple other opponents within a 3 tile radius (forming a 7x7 square centred on the target) at the moment the special attack is initiated.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1356679120306995201|date=2 February 2021|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/96437|archivedate=23 April 2023|quote=It does it all on the tick when the attack took place. It's always a radius of 3 tiles.}} ''Chainhit'' can only ricochet to other enemies in a [[multicombat area]]. This special attack can target up to 5 opponents, but a rune thrownaxe will not rebound back to its original target after chaining to another enemy.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1356223483261423619|date=1 February 2021|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/100258|archivedate=23 April 2023|quote=The game makes a list of potential targets in a random order, ensuring that it doesn't include the original target. The randomness may be skewed rather than evenly distributed; I can't tell exactly what the engine's doing under the hood. It's not doing tactical routeing, anyway.}} Furthermore, an accuracy roll is made after each target hit by the thrownaxe, which will end the sequence if failed.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1336282813272829952|date=8 December 2020|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/97304|archivedate=25 April 2023|quote=The thing that picks nearby targets does it randomly, and has a chance at each step of stopping (by failing an accuracy roll) rather than moving on to another.}} Each hit consumes 10% of the player's special attack energy, i.e. three hits consumes 30% energy. This special attack uses a custom form of damage calculation that does not take into account offensive Prayers (such as [[Eagle Eye]]) when determining a player's [[Maximum ranged hit#Calculating your maximum ranged hit|maximum Ranged hit]].{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1167133164642480131|date=29 August 2019|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/102769|archivedate=23 May 2021|quote=[The special attack for rune throwing axes, magic shortbow, and magic longbow all ignore the active range boosting prayers for the special attack, and this ends up with a lower max hit during special attack. is this intentional?] They always had their own special calculations for effective accuracy/damage (...)}} In addition, the attack speed of ''Chainhit'' is one tick slower than the rune thrownaxe's regular attack, meaning the attack interval is 5 ticks (3.0s) on the rapid style, and 6 ticks (3.6s) on accurate or longrange. The following formula can be used to calculate the max hit of ''Chainhit'':

\text{Maximum Hit} =\left\lfloor 0.5+\frac{(\text{Visible Ranged Level} + 10) *(\text{Ammo Ranged Strength} +64)}{640} \right\rfloor {{efn|name=level|Only the player's visible ranged level matters. Boosts given through prayers and Void Knight armour are ignored.}} {{efn|name=stance|A stance bonus of 10 is used regardless of attack style used.}} {{efn|name=ammo|The ranged strength bonus of the player's armour and gear is ignored. Only the ranged strength bonus of the rune thrownaxe is used.}} {{efn|name=slayer|The bonus damage usually provided by wearing a Slayer helmet (i), Salve amulet (i) or Salve amulet (ei) is ignored.}} {{notelist}} ==Item sources== {{Drop sources|Rune thrownaxe}} ===Shop locations=== {{Store locations list|Rune thrownaxe}} ==Used in recommended equipment== {{Used in recommended equipment}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 14 June 2004 |update = Special Attacks |change = The item was given a special attack, ''Chainhit''. }} {{Subject changes |date = 29 March 2004 |update = RS2 Launched! |change = The item became permanently available with the launch of ''RuneScape 2''. }} {{Subject changes |date = 21 January 2004 |update = More RS2 Updates |change = The item became obtainable in the ''RuneScape 2'' Beta. }} {{Subject changes |date = 1 December 2003 |update = RuneScape 2 Beta is Here! |change = The item was added to the ''RuneScape 2'' Beta cache. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ==Trivia== *They are one of only two rune metal weapons to have a special attack, the other being the [[rune claws]]. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ranged weapons}} {{Rune equipment}} [[Category:Weapons with Special attacks]]
